##NAMES=Powellite

##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca(MoO_4_)
##LOCALITY=Carlota mine, Pinto Creek, Gila, Arizona, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Joe Ruiz
##DESCRIPTION=Pale green thick tabular crystals, associated with blue azurite, green elongated szenicsite (R100179) and colorless granular prosopite (R100181). 
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R100180
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.239 b: 5.239 c: 11.457 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 314.49 crystal system: tetragonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
